questions.stem: For a unity feedback closed loop system with forward path transfer function G(s) = K(s-2)/(s+1)^2, draw the Nyquist plot (assuming K=1).
common_errors: Missing the open-RHP zero at s=2 and its effect on encirclements
Incorrectly applying Nyquist stability criterion when P (OL poles in RHP) is nonzero or RHP zeros are present
Not correctly drawing the mirror image (negative frequency) portion of the plot
Forgetting to account for the indentation around any jω-axis poles
questions.solution_method: Substitute s=jω into G(jω)=K(jω-2)/(jω+1)^2 and evaluate magnitude and phase for ω from 0 to ∞, then mirror for −∞ to 0, and close at infinity. Count net clockwise encirclements N of −1/K point; use Z=N+P to find RHP closed-loop poles.
key_formulas: Z = N + P (Nyquist: Z=CL RHP poles, N=CW encirclements, P=OL RHP poles)

questions.stem: For the same unity feedback system G(s) = K(s-2)/(s+1)^2, determine the condition on K for closed-loop stability using the Nyquist plot.
common_errors: Forgetting that G(s) has a RHP zero (non-minimum phase) which affects the Nyquist encirclement count
Applying Bode gain-margin logic instead of encirclement-counting logic
Sign error in computing the real-axis crossing of the Nyquist plot
questions.solution_method: G(s) has P=0 open-loop RHP poles (poles at s=-1, multiplicity 2) and one RHP zero at s=2. For stability Z=0 we need N=0. Find where Nyquist plot crosses real axis (set Im[G(jω)]=0, solve for ω_cross), evaluate Re[G(jω_cross)], and require −1/K not encircled, giving a bound on K.
key_formulas: Stability: K < 1/|Re[G(jω_cross)]| (direction depends on sign of real-axis crossing)
Z = N + P = 0 required for stability

questions.stem: For a closed loop system with loop transfer function G(s)H(s) = e^{-sT} / [s(s+1)], determine the range of dead time T such that the closed loop system remains stable.
common_errors: Forgetting that dead time e^{-jωT} contributes only phase shift (−ωT rad) without changing magnitude
Using gain crossover frequency from the no-delay system incorrectly
Not expressing the stability condition as T < π/(2ω_gc) correctly
questions.solution_method: Find gain crossover frequency ω_gc of 1/[s(s+1)] (magnitude=1), giving ω_gc=1 rad/s (approximately). Phase of G(jω)H(jω) at ω_gc is −90°−atan(ω_gc)−ω_gc·T. For stability, total phase must be > −180°, so −90°−45°−ω_gc·T > −180°, yielding T < 45°/ω_gc in radians → T < π/4 seconds.

notes: Tutorial dated 21 April 2026. Q1 includes an indicative Nyquist diagram (K=1) showing the plot for the non-minimum-phase system. Q1 split into 1A (draw) and 1B (stability condition). Both questions are Nyquist/frequency-domain stability problems; Q2 involves dead-time (transport lag) — tests phase-margin reasoning under delay.
